rationale_for_baseline_tier: A benign but symptomatic seborrhoeic dermatitis flare with anterior blepharitis — prompt prescription and lid hygiene with a clear ocular/periorbital safety-net (a slit-lamp exam cannot be done remotely); not an emergency now.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Severe eye pain, sensitivity to light, or blurred/reduced vision
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get severe eye pain, sensitivity to light, or your vision blurs, go to an eye emergency department straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Hot, hard, spreading facial/periorbital swelling with fever, or clustered punched-out sores
escalation_edges.rationale: Periorbital cellulitis or eczema herpeticum are emergencies.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the swelling around your eye becomes hot, hard and spreading with a fever, or you get clusters of small punched-out sores, go to the emergency department.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Provide clear reassurance and a specific ocular safety-net; support the psychosocial impact.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This is seborrhoeic dermatitis with eyelid involvement. Use the medicated shampoo on your scalp/face, a mild steroid cream on the face for a few days only, and warm-compress lid hygiene twice daily. Watch your eyes closely.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: GP within days for prescription/review; eye emergency for severe eye symptoms; ED for periorbital cellulitis features
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: After-hours GP for the prescription; eye ED for severe eye pain/vision change; ED for hot/hard periorbital swelling.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document seborrhoeic dermatitis with anterior blepharitis (keratitis/periorbital cellulitis/eczema herpeticum screened negative), antifungal + short low-potency steroid + lid hygiene, and an ocular safety-net.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring/treating without an ocular or periorbital safety-net.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A herpetic keratitis or periorbital cellulitis is missed and threatens sight/life.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Routine handling without lid hygiene/treatment or an eye safety-net.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Emergency ambulance for uncomplicated seborrhoeic dermatitis/blepharitis without ocular red flags.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot perform a slit-lamp/fluorescein exam remotely to exclude keratitis.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the seborrhoeic dermatitis/blepharitis and give a clear ocular/periorbital safety-net with a low threshold for eye assessment.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Falsely reassuring the eye is fine, or overusing facial steroids.
